event: Neolithic farming communities of the Starcevo and later Vucedol cultures settle along the eastern Adriatic and Pannonian plains.
Illyrian tribes establish settlements across the region, becoming the dominant population of the eastern Adriatic.
Greek colonists found settlements such as Issa (Vis) and Pharos (Hvar) on the Adriatic islands.
Rome launches the first of the Illyrian Wars, beginning its conquest of the eastern Adriatic coast.
Rome completes its conquest, organizing the region into the provinces of Dalmatia and Pannonia.
Emperor Diocletian completes his vast retirement palace at Split, the core of the modern city.
Slavic and Avar raids destroy the Roman city of Salona, scattering refugees to Diocletian's Palace at Split.
Croat tribes migrate into the region of Dalmatia and Pannonia, settling among the local population.
Croatian lands come under Frankish influence as Charlemagne's empire expands into the Adriatic.
Duke Branimir is recognized by Pope John VIII, marking papal acknowledgment of an independent Croatian duchy.
Tomislav unites the Croatian lands and is traditionally regarded as the first king of Croatia.
Peter Kresimir IV expands the medieval Croatian kingdom to its greatest territorial extent.
Croatia enters a personal union with Hungary under King Coloman through the Pacta Conventa.
King Bela IV grants Zagreb's Gradec the Golden Bull, making it a free royal city amid the Mongol invasion.
The Treaty of Zadar forces Venice to cede control of Dalmatia to the Hungarian-Croatian crown.
Venice purchases Dalmatia from Ladislaus of Naples, beginning centuries of Venetian rule over the coast.
Ottoman forces crush the Croatian nobility at the Battle of Krbava Field.
Croatian nobles elect Ferdinand I of Habsburg as king at the Parliament of Cetin, beginning Habsburg rule.
The Croatian-Slovenian peasant revolt led by Matija Gubec is brutally suppressed.
Christian forces defeat the Ottomans at the Battle of Sisak, halting their advance into Croatia.
The Zrinski-Frankopan conspiracy against Habsburg rule ends with the execution of its leaders in Vienna.
Napoleon incorporates Croatian and Dalmatian territories into the French Illyrian Provinces.
Ljudevit Gaj launches the Croatian National Revival, the Illyrian Movement promoting Croatian language and identity.
Ban Josip Jelacic leads Croatian forces and abolishes serfdom amid the revolutions sweeping the Habsburg Empire.
The Croatian-Hungarian Settlement (Nagodba) defines Croatia's autonomous status within the Kingdom of Hungary.
Croatia joins the Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes as the Austro-Hungarian Empire collapses.
Croatian Peasant Party leader Stjepan Radic is fatally shot in the Yugoslav parliament, deepening ethnic tensions.
King Alexander imposes a royal dictatorship and renames the country the Kingdom of Yugoslavia.
The Cvetkovic-Macek Agreement creates an autonomous Banovina of Croatia within Yugoslavia.
Axis powers invade Yugoslavia and install the fascist Ustasha-led Independent State of Croatia.
Tito's Partisans liberate Croatia, which becomes a constituent republic of socialist Yugoslavia.
The Croatian Spring movement demanding greater autonomy is suppressed by the Yugoslav leadership.
Croatia holds its first multi-party elections, won by Franjo Tudjman's HDZ amid rising independence sentiment.
Croatia declares independence from Yugoslavia, triggering the Croatian War of Independence.
Serb forces besiege and shell the historic walled city of Dubrovnik and devastate Vukovar.
Croatia gains international recognition and is admitted to the United Nations.
Operations Flash and Storm reclaim most occupied territory, effectively ending the war of independence.
Croatia peacefully reintegrates Eastern Slavonia, completing the restoration of its territorial integrity.
President Franjo Tudjman dies, ushering in a more reformist, pro-Western political era.
Croatia joins NATO, anchoring itself in the Western security alliance.
Croatia becomes the 28th member state of the European Union.
Croatia adopts the euro as its currency and joins the Schengen passport-free travel area.
